body.Subject: International R&D Scholar Outreach, Thesis, and Funding-Opportunity Expectations
body.Body:
@{concat('

Dear ', triggerFormDataValue('first_name'), ',

Thank you for applying to the US Fellows International R&D Scholar pathway. The goal is to build a credible research network around a clear thesis or technical question—not to seek a generic endorsement.

Prepare the research package

  • A concise thesis or project summary, research question, objectives, methods direction, and intended impact.
  • Your institution, degree context, home advisor, and preferably an advisor endorsement.
  • Your CV, relevant publications, portfolio, and existing endorsements.
  • Several relevant solicitation IDs or official opportunity links.
  • One specific request for each recipient.

A network may include your home advisor, a field-aligned research scientist, a U.S. higher-education researcher, an industry or mission partner, and other relevant experts. Never list a person or organization without explicit permission.

Verify opportunities

Review Grants.gov, SAM.gov, SBIR, STTR, fellowships, graduate assistantships, cooperative agreements, and industry-sponsored research. Confirm topic, deadline, eligibility, deliverables, and eligible lead-organization rules in the official notice.

Make a specific ask

You may request thesis or methods feedback, an introduction, solicitation guidance, an endorsement, or consideration of an SME, Key Personnel, PI, or Co-PI role for a named opportunity. Personalize the request and avoid mass outreach.

Protect the work

Do not share classified, export-controlled, proprietary, unpublished sensitive, or protected personal information. A conversation or endorsement does not imply supervision, employment, funding, sponsorship, proposal participation, laboratory access, or institutional commitment.

Keep written records of endorsements, permissions, and agreed roles. The complete guide is available at usfellows.org/international-rd-scholars.html.

US Fellows

')}
